seo.title: Building Research Agents with LangGraph + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with LangGraph + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: LangGraph enables building sophisticated Research Agents as stateful graphs for multi-step web search, analysis, and synthesis workflows. These cyclical agents handle complex tasks like information gathering and summarization but are prone to failures like infinite loops and state corruption. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify source accuracy, claim validity, recency, and bias, ensuring reliable outputs in production environments.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with LangGraph
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents in LangGraph are structured as directed graphs with nodes representing specialized agents: a Router for task decomposition, Search Agent for web queries, Analyzer for fact-checking, Synthesizer for summarization, and optional Human-in-the-loop for approval. Edges define conditional flows based on state, such as re-searching if confidence is low or escalating to human review. Checkpoints persist state across cycles, enabling long-running research sessions.

The workflow typically starts with a user query, routes to parallel search branches (web, academic, news), merges results with conflict resolution, then analyzes for accuracy and bias before final synthesis. This cyclical design supports iterative refinement until quality thresholds are met.

State schema includes messages, sources, confidence scores, and metadata, with custom reducers for merging parallel branch outputs.

risks.name: Cyclic graph execution causing infinite state transitions
risks.description: Router conditions fail to exit research loop when search confidence never meets threshold
risks.mitigation: Add Vex verification node with max_iterations state check before conditional edges

risks.name: State corruption when parallel branches merge conflicting data
risks.description: Search agents return contradictory facts that corrupt summary state during merge
risks.mitigation: Use Vex source verification during state reduction to resolve conflicts

risks.name: Checkpoint deserialization errors after schema changes
risks.description: Adding confidence_score field breaks loaded checkpoints from previous runs
risks.mitigation: Implement Vex schema validation wrapper around graph checkpoints

risks.name: Human-in-the-loop breakpoints bypassed by conditional edges
risks.description: Low-confidence states route to auto-retry instead of human review
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces human-in-loop for medium-risk outputs regardless of graph routing

implementation.title: Basic Research Agent Graph
implementation.description: Core LangGraph structure with router, search, and synthesizer nodes
implementation.code:
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages

from langchain_community.tools.tavily_search import TavilySearchResults

class ResearchState(TypedDict):
    messages: Annotated[list, add_messages]
    sources: list[str]
    confidence: float
    summary: str

    
# Simplified nodes
def router(state: ResearchState) -> str:
    return "search" if state['confidence'] < 0.8 else "synthesize"

def search_node(state: ResearchState) -> ResearchState:
    tool = TavilySearchResults(max_results=3)
    return {"sources": tool.run(state['messages'][-1].content), "confidence": 0.7}

def synthesize_node(state: ResearchState) -> ResearchState:
    state['summary'] = f"Summary based on {len(state['sources'])} sources"
    return state

# Build graph
graph = StateGraph(ResearchState)
graph.add_node("router", router)
graph.add_node("search", search_node)
graph.add_node("synthesize", synthesize_node)
graph.add_conditional_edges("router", router, {"search": "search", "synthesize": "synthesize"})
graph.set_entry_point("router")
research_graph = graph.compile(checkpointer=MemorySaver())

implementation.title: Parallel Search Branches
implementation.description: Multiple search agents running concurrently with state merging
implementation.code:
from langgraph.prebuilt import ToolNode
from typing_extensions import Literal

# Add parallel edges
graph.add_node("web_search", web_search_node)
graph.add_node("academic_search", academic_search_node)

def merge_sources(state: ResearchState) -> ResearchState:
    all_sources = state['sources_web'] + state['sources_academic']
    state['sources'] = list(set(all_sources))  # Dedupe
    return state

graph.add_node("merge", merge_sources)
graph.add_edge("router", "web_search")
graph.add_edge("router", "academic_search")
graph.add_edge("web_search", "merge")
graph.add_edge("academic_search", "merge")
graph.add_edge("merge", "analyze")

implementation.title: State Schema with Confidence Tracking
implementation.code:
class VerifiedResearchState(TypedDict):
    messages: Annotated[list, add_messages]
    sources: list[dict]  # {url, snippet, recency_score}
    claims: list[dict]   # {text, confidence, evidence}
    confidence: float
    bias_score: float
    needs_human_review: bool
    iteration_count: int

    
def update_confidence(state: VerifiedResearchState) -> VerifiedResearchState:
    avg_conf = sum(c['confidence'] for c in state['claims']) / len(state['claims'])
    state['confidence'] = min(avg_conf, 1.0)
    state['needs_human_review'] = state['confidence'] < 0.7
    state['iteration_count'] += 1
    return state

implementation.title: Human-in-the-Loop Integration

vexIntegration.title: Wrap graph.invoke() with Vex.verify()
Vex as Graph Verification Node
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as intermediate node before synthesis
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Client
vex_client = Client(api_key="your-vex-key")

@vex_node
def vex_verification_node(state: VerifiedResearchState) -> VerifiedResearchState:
    response = vex_client.verify(
        text=state['summary'],
        context={
            "sources": state['sources'],
            "confidence": state['confidence'],
            "iteration": state['iteration_count']
        },
        checks=[
            "source_verification",
            "claim_accuracy",
            "recency_checks",
            "bias_detection"
        ]
    )
    
    state['vex_score'] = response.score
    state['vex_issues'] = response.issues
    state['confidence'] = min(state['confidence'], response.score)
    
    if response.status != "approved":
        state['needs_human_review'] = True
    
    return state

graph.add_node("vex_verify", vex_verification_node)
graph.add_edge("analyze", "vex_verify")
graph.add_conditional_edges("vex_verify", 
    lambda s: "human_review" if s['needs_human_review'] else "synthesize")

vexIntegration.title: Prevent Infinite Loops with Vex Circuit Breaker

cta.heading: Secure Your LangGraph Research Agents
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and protect your research agents from hallucinations, bias, and infinite loops.
